<!-- BRANDS -->
<section class="brands" id="brands">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-eyebrow">The Makers</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Brands We <em>Carry</em></h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="brand-grid">

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Tokai"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Tokai</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1947 · Hamamatsu</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Tokai's "lawsuit-era" guitars of the late '70s and '80s are legendary for rivalling — and often surpassing — their American counterparts. Their Love Rock and Springy Sound series remain benchmark instruments for tone and build quality.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Navigator"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Navigator</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1977 · ESP Japan</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Navigator is ESP Japan's premium domestic brand — guitars built for professional players who demand nothing short of perfection. Exceptional fit, finish, and hardware set these apart from anything in a similar price bracket.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Greco"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Greco</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1960 · Kanda Shokai</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">One of Japan's most storied guitar brands, Greco produced some of the finest Les Paul and Stratocaster copies ever made during the golden years of Japanese manufacturing. A vintage Greco is a collector's treasure.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="ESP"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">ESP</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1975 · Tokyo</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Founded in Tokyo, ESP's Japan-made instruments are the choice of professional artists worldwide. Built in their domestic factories to exacting standards, ESP Japan guitars represent the pinnacle of the brand's craft and heritage.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Fender Made in Japan"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Fender Japan</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1982 · Fuji Gen</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Born from a collaboration between Fender and Fuji Gen, Fender Japan produced some of the most beloved Stratocasters and Telecasters outside of the USA. The craftsmanship and quality control of these instruments is second to none.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Momose"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Momose</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 2004 · Deviser Group</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">The pinnacle of modern Japanese guitar making. Momose instruments are handcrafted to exacting specifications in Matsumoto, using premium tonewoods and hardware. Each guitar is a masterwork of contemporary luthiery.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Moon Guitars"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Moon Guitars</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1978 · Osaka</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Moon Guitars has earned a cult following among bass and guitar players for their exceptional playability and refined aesthetics. Handbuilt in Japan with meticulous attention to detail, every Moon instrument is a statement of quiet excellence.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Combat Guitars"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Combat Guitars</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1995 · Tokyo</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">A boutique Tokyo builder known for extraordinary custom work and finishing. Combat guitars are hand-assembled by skilled craftspeople and highly sought after by collectors who appreciate exceptional Japanese artisanship beyond the mainstream brands.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

    <div class="brand-card">
      <div class="brand-logo-wrap"><img class="brand-logo-img" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Crews Maniac Sound"></div>
      <div class="brand-name">Crews Maniac Sound</div>
      <div class="brand-era">Est. 1982 · Japan</div>
      <p class="brand-desc">Crews Maniac Sound builds some of Japan's most beautifully crafted instruments — revered by session musicians and serious collectors alike. Their meticulous approach to wood selection and construction yields guitars of extraordinary resonance and character.</p>
      <a class="brand-link" href="#contact">View inventory</a>
    </div>

<!-- WHY JAPAN -->
<section class="why">
  <div class="why-bg-kanji">匠</div>
  <div class="why-inner">
    <div class="why-left">
      <div class="section-eyebrow">Why Japanese Guitars</div>
      <h2 class="section-title">The Art of<br><em>Takumi</em></h2>
      <div class="section-rule" style="margin-left:0;"></div>
      <p>Japan's guitar industry grew from obsessive attention to craft. What began as faithful copies of American classics evolved into something uniquely Japanese — instruments of astonishing quality, built by craftspeople who take immense pride in their work.</p>
      <p style="margin-top:1rem;">Tsunami Guitars exists to bring these instruments to players around the world who know that the finest guitars don't always come from the most famous factories.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="why-pillars">
      <div class="pillar">
        <div class="pillar-num">01</div>
        <div class="pillar-content">
          <h3>Takumi — Mastery</h3>
          <p>Japanese craftspeople train for years before touching a production instrument. The concept of Takumi — devoted mastery — runs through every guitar that leaves a Japanese factory.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="pillar">
        <div class="pillar-num">02</div>
        <div class="pillar-content">
          <h3>Exceptional Quality Control</h3>
          <p>Japanese factories are renowned for tolerances that exceed American and European counterparts. Setup, fretwork, and finishing are consistently outstanding across every price point.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="pillar">
        <div class="pillar-num">03</div>
        <div class="pillar-content">
          <h3>Undervalued & Overlooked</h3>
          <p>A vintage Tokai or Greco can outplay a US vintage guitar at a fraction of the price. These instruments are among the best value propositions in the entire guitar world.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="pillar">
        <div class="pillar-num">04</div>
        <div class="pillar-content">
          <h3>A Living Tradition</h3>
          <p>Modern builders like Momose and Deviser prove that Japan's golden age of guitar making never ended — it evolved. Today's Japanese guitars are among the finest anywhere on earth.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
